comments.text: I know people call the Sahara “empty,” but after watching this, I honestly think that is the biggest misunderstanding about the desert. It feels more alive and mysterious than most modern citie

comments.author: @gabyherrmann9894
comments.text: The Sahara is my 2. homeland. More than 35 years I am in connection with the Sahara.  With a 4x4 you are able to visit the most beautiful landscapes, scenery, you can't imagine. The secrets and mysterious  phenomenas are incredible. I am 80 years old. And I have to do it again and again. ❤❤❤

replies.author: @gabyherrmann9894
replies.text: ​@trueglobe66 Thanks for nice words. The most beautiful landscape was at the border of Lybia south to Chad. Dohone - Enneri Thon. I can't describe all these unique   mountains, needles of rocks, colours of sands, arches, fossilized trees. Unfortunately all these regions are not possible to explore any more. I am from Germany and drive a Toyota 78 special equiped for Desert.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text:
That is beautiful to read. ❤️ Having more than thirty-five years of connection with the Sahara is something truly special. The desert must feel very different when someone knows it not as a tourist, but almost as a second homeland.
What is the one place or moment in the Sahara that stayed in your heart the most after all these years?

comments.text: The Sahara is far more than endless sand dunes. From hidden oases and ancient trade routes to dramatic rock formations and unique cultures, it reveals a side of the desert that many people never get to see. A fascinating journey into one of Earth's most misunderstood landscapes.
